title: Chef Craft Select Plastic Measuring Cup, 1 Cup Capacity, Clear
description:
About this item
Clear plastic for durability and ease of measuring
Both US and metric graduations to suit any recipe
Easy to read graduations
Can be used to measure liquids or powders
Dishwasher safe
reviews_1s:
I loved its initial performance. And then: surface got cloudy. And, critically, star-shaped mini-cracks developed at the middle of the base. These became real leaks within a year of use. Unacceptable!

I worked as a researcher. 40-years ago, there were plastics that would last for years and years in a harsh environment: acids, bases, etcetera. The technology exists. A product like this is simply shamefulArrived shattered. Don’t know if it was the packaging, the handling in transit, or the quality of the item that caused the problem.I had bought one before and it was fine. So then I bought two more. I think they changed the material to a cheaper plastic or used a more wretched sticker adhesive. The giant sticker on the cup is really hard to remove. I managed to get it peeled off with much trouble, but it left some adhesive residue. So I got out the lemon oil that I usually use to remove adhesive residue. That was a disaster! The lemon oil seems to have damaged the surface of the plastic making it hazy. Then I tried to remove the haze with rubbing (isopropyl) alcohol. It did no good on the haze but it did remove a little of the printing on the cup!

The moral of the story - DO NOT BUY THIS CUP! The sticker is gigantic and ridiculously difficult to remove. Common solvents one might use to remove the sticker adhesive seen to attack either the plastic itself or the printing on the cup. My earlier version of the same cup did not seem to have this problem. They either changed the sticker adhesive or went to a different cheaper and less resistant plastic. When they just say “plastic” without telling what plastic like acrylic or polycarbonate which is what should be used for such an item it means it’s a cheap plastic unsuitable for the purpose. And the ridiculously large non-removeable sticker is the final problem!number have been smudged off. cant tell the measurementDo not buy this junk! It has multiple cracks after two months of use . Shame on Amazon for selling low grade plastic productsI spent 7 dollars on this measuring cup thinking it was going to be bigger when i saw how small it was when it came in the mail. I read all of the reviews and everything first and realized I forgot to check the size before ordering which was my fault. Secondly, I looked up microwave safe measuring cups and this came up. Cool, got this one. It didn't hold up. Don't order if you need it for that reason. I should've just went with glass in the first place like I was going to.You get what you paid for -- cheap! Have you noticed that there aren't any marking on the bottom of what kind of plastic it's made from? No recycle symbol with a number in it? Who knows what kind of plastic it is, but it isn't food safe.

The first time I used it, I was making a lemon cake that required lemon rind as one of the ingredients. I grated the outside of my lemon and put it in the cup to measure the amount. I left it there for 20 minutes while I was preparing the rest of the ingredients. When I went to add the lemon rind I poured it in and noticed the cup was all scratched up. I thought it was wax from the lemon rind. After I washed it, the cup never got clean again. I inspected it closely and saw that the plastic was actually melted off. The acid from the lemon did that! I finished the cake and baked it, but then got paranoid about melted plastic that might have gotten into my cake. I ended up throwing the whole thing out. What a waste of my time and ingredients! I also threw out the cup and ordered something more durable.Terrible product. I bought this to replace my old plastic 2 cup measuring cup that I used to measure my dog's dry food. I had that measuring cup for well over 10 years and could no longer read the measuring lines as they were worn off. Bought this one and the handle broke within one month while scooping out dog food from the bin. I usually try not to use plastic but never want a glass measuring cup to break by mistake into my dog's food bin. It's crap... don't buy.I bought three of these and all three fell apart in a short timeMade mistake of putting it in microwave plastic was affected
